description: 52-week AI plan generation with current-date-aware dashboard, real grass photos in onboarding picker, and soil-temperature-based pre-emergent timing banners

userStories.title: Soil temperature pre-emergent data by state
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a data file mapping each US state to its average spring and fall pre-emergent application windows so the banner and plan can use accurate timing without any external API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/data/preemergent-windows.ts
Export a PREEMERGENT_WINDOWS record keyed by two-letter uppercase state code (e.g. 'NC')
Each entry has: spring: { startDate: 'MM-DD', endDate: 'MM-DD' } and fall: { startDate: 'MM-DD', endDate: 'MM-DD' }
Spring window = typical date range soil reaches 50°F; fall window = typical date range soil reaches 70°F
Export a getPreemergentWindow(state: string) function returning the entry or null for unknown states
All 50 US states are covered (group by climate zone — see PRD technical notes for zone assignments)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Upgrade API to 52-week plan generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the /api/parse-pdf route to return a 52-week plan instead of a 12-month plan so the dashboard can show week-precise tasks using today's date.
acceptanceCriteria: Rename MonthlyPlan interface to WeeklyPlan: { week: number; tasks: WeeklyTask[] } where week is 1–52
Rename MonthlyTask interface to WeeklyTask (same fields: title, description, category, priority, petSafetyNote)
Update the Claude system prompt to request exactly 52 entries (weeks 1–52) instead of 12 months
Prompt must instruct Claude to factor in state and grassType for seasonally correct week-level timing
Response JSON array has exactly 52 entries; weeks with no tasks use tasks: []
pdfUrl parameter is now optional — when empty string or omitted, skip the URL fetch and pass state/grassType directly to Claude for knowledge-based generation
Validation: require state and grassType but not pdfUrl (return 400 only if state or grassType are missing)
DB cache upsert remains unchanged (ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE) — old 12-month entries are replaced on next fetch
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Dashboard 'This Week' card using current date
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to show exactly what I should do on my lawn this week, not just this month, based on today's date.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a getISOWeek(date: Date): number utility (see PRD for ISO week math using UTC) — do NOT use a library
Dashboard calls /api/parse-pdf with { state, grassType } (no pdfUrl) and receives the 52-week WeeklyPlan[]
Use getISOWeek(new Date()) to find the matching week entry in the plan
The subtitle below the heading changes from just month name to the week date range (e.g. 'Your lawn: Bermudagrass • June 9–15')
Compute the week date range from the ISO week number and current year (Monday–Sunday)
Tasks sort urgent → routine → optional as before
If the current week has no tasks, show the existing empty-state message
Pet safety notes still display when has_pets is true
The sqFt quantity-scaling regex logic is preserved unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Pre-curate real grass type photo URLs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the grassTypes data to include real publicly accessible photo URLs so the onboarding picker can display them without a runtime API call.
acceptanceCriteria: Add photoCredit: string field to the GrassType interface in src/data/grass-types.ts
Replace all 10 photoUrl values (currently /images/grass/*.jpg placeholders) with real CDN URLs from Pexels or Unsplash
Pexels format: https://images.pexels.com/photos/[ID]/pexels-photo-[ID].jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&w=640
Unsplash format: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[ID]?w=640&q=80
Each photo must clearly show the blade texture/color/growth pattern characteristic of that specific grass species — not a generic lawn
All 10 grass types have a working URL and a populated photoCredit string (e.g. 'Photo by Jane Doe on Pexels')
Add images.pexels.com and/or images.unsplash.com to remotePatterns in next.config.ts so next/image can load them
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Grass photo picker in onboarding
userStories.description: As a new user selecting my grass type, I want to see a real photo of each variety so I can visually confirm my grass before selecting it.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify which onboarding step currently renders the grass-type selector (check src/app/onboarding/[step]/steps/Step*.tsx files)
Replace the existing text-only list with a card grid: each card shows the grass photo (top ~60% of card via next/image) and name + up to 3 trait badges (bottom ~40%)
Grid layout: grid-cols-2 on mobile, grid-cols-3 on md: breakpoint
Selected card gets a 2px border in var(--color-primary) color; unselected cards get the existing #2d4a2d border
Use next/image with the photoUrl from grassTypes data; add width and height or use fill with a relative parent
Show a small muted attribution line below each photo using the photoCredit field
Tapping/clicking a card selects that grass type and the Continue button enables
Previously selected grass type (from sessionStorage onboarding_state.grass_type) is pre-selected on mount
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Pre-emergent timing banner on dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want a prominent alert when my pre-emergent window is 3 weeks away or currently active so I don't miss the critical timing.
acceptanceCriteria: Import getPreemergentWindow from src/data/preemergent-windows.ts in the dashboard page
After reading state from sessionStorage, call getPreemergentWindow(state) to get spring and fall windows
Compute whether today falls within 21 days before OR during either the spring or fall window

If yes, render a banner above the task list with: seasonal icon (🌱 spring, 🍂 fall), headline ('Pre-Emergent Window: X weeks away' or 'Pre-Emergent Window: Active Now'), sub-line ('Apply when soil temp reaches 50°F (spring) or 70°F (fall). Typical window for [STATE]: [startDate] – [endDate]'), and a dismiss button (×)
Banner background: #3d3000, text: #eab308 (matching existing pet safety note style)
Dismiss button sets a sessionStorage flag 'preemergent_banner_dismissed' = 'true' and hides the banner
On mount, check the sessionStorage flag — if set, do not show the banner
If neither spring nor fall window is within 21 days, render nothing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
